---
title: SoilWise HE recap
subtitle: "ISRIC - World Soil Information"
author: Paul van Genuchten
date: 2026-06-10
format:
  revealjs: 
    theme: serif
    logo: https://omgeving.vlaanderen.be/sites/default/files/2025-10/SWlogocolor.svg
    footer: Soilwise HE - <a href="https://cordis.europa.eu/project/id/101112838">EU Horizon Grant 101112838</a>
---


## SoilWise-HE in Context

A Horizon Europe - Mission Soil - project (2024-2027)

* SoilWise aims to become a long-term European catalogue for soil knowledge and data.
* Focus on discoverability rather than replacing existing repositories.
* Harvests metadata from distributed sources.
* Intended as a future building block for the European Soil Observatory (EUSO).

## Core challenge

Europe's soil information is fragmented across:

* Academic repositories
* Project websites / portals / knowledge hubs
* Governmental data catalogues
* 3rd party platforms (youtube, linkedin, github)

Goal: provide one integrated access point while respecting ownership and governance. Encourage sustainable publication, instead of refactoring existing publications.

## SoilWise Technical Components

1. Metadata harvesting
3. Catalogue
4. Metadata validation
5. Metadata authoring
6. Metadata transformation & harmonisation
7. Metadata augmentation
8. Knowledge graph
9. Natural language querying
10. Data publication guidance
11. Data harmonisation

## Diagram

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    S1[Source 1] -->|harvest| h[(Ingested)]
    S2[Source 2] -->|harvest| h[(Ingested)]
    h --> HM{harmonise}
    HM --> h2[(Harmonised)]
    h2 --> AU{augment}
    shkg[(Knowledge Graph)] --> AU
    AU --> h3[(Search index)]
```

## EUSO will not store (harmonized) data

SoilWise primarily indexes metadata rather than replicating datasets.

* lower storage requirements
* respect data ownership
* support distributed repositories
* easier integration of heterogeneous sources

## Typical harvested sources

* Government (INSPIRE / data.europa.eu)
* Academia (CORDIS / OpenAire)
* EO portals (Copernicus)
* Meta repositories / knowledge hubs


## Harmonising heterogeneous sources

Repositories expose different schemas:

* Dublin Core / DCAT
* ISO 19139 / INSPIRE metadata
* DataCite / ARC / RO-Crate
* Repository-specific profiles

## Metadata Augmentation

SoilWise enriches records using:

* NLP
* scripts
* rule engines
* LLM-assisted approaches

Increase FAIRness without modifying the original source record.

## Example augmentations

* inferred keywords
* inferred spatial scope
* ontology mappings
* thematic classifications

## Linking metadata to shared vocabularies

Metadata augmentation connects records to:

* AGROVOC
* GEMET
* Soil terminology resources (GLOSIS/Glosolan/WRB)

## Benefits

* multilingual discovery
* synonym handling
* semantic search
* cross-repository interoperability

## SoilWise SHKG

* Derived from selected documents using llm
* SKOS-centred
* linked to external vocabularies
* connected to soil-health indicators

## [w3id.org/EUSoilVoc](https://w3id.org/eusoilvoc)

- A vocabulary to facilitate data harmonization
- A hierarchy of soil properties and observation procedures
- Inspired by Ansis Field and lab guide vocabulary
- Challenges on how to improve the content

## Data harmonisation

- Exploring annotations of tabular data using RO-Crate / CSVW
- Geopackage format to snapshot soil observation systems
- Flows to transform between formats (csv, rdf, geopackage, gml)
- Vizualise the possibilities of creating and using integrated datasets, by linking soil data to other domains 

## Tabular data annotation

![annotate a csv](img/tabbular_annotated.png)

## Tabular data annotation technologies

- schema.org
- CSVW
- RO-crate
- Frictionless data
- ISO19110 Feature cataloguing

## SOSA

![SOSA structure](img/sosa.png)

## GeoPackage as a soil data exchange format

- A relational database model oriented on the ISO28258/INSPIRE Soil database model
- For operational soil information systems, a dump of the database at intervals could be facilitated using geopackage
- The geopackage can act as a backend for an offline surveying app

## Remaining challenges

- Aggregated repositories offer vast amount of records, ingesting relevant items is a challenge, requires specific filters / user suggestions
- Ontology alignment / Soil terminology harmonisation
- Post project institutionalisation

## Outlook

* Richer metadata augmentation
* Expanded knowledge graph
* AI-assisted discovery and reasoning
